payload.summary: All three amendments are verified against the actual codebase and identify genuine bugs. Amendments fr-1-0b213bd9 and fr-1-929f7a9f address the same root cause (unconditional plan_hash auto-population at the service boundary via `.or_else(|| snapshot.plan_hash.clone())` breaks idempotent replay after `persist_plan` advances `snapshot.plan_hash`) from complementary angles — one focuses on the adapter-level terminal replay conflict check, the other on moving the auto-population to be conditional on the matched row lacking plan_hash. Amendment fr-1-b656ac14 identifies a confirmed missing `started_at` guard in the runless fallback path that violates the explicitly documented service contract.

votes.rationale: Verified in code. The auto-population at service.rs:429-433 and 564-568 uses `plan_hash.or_else(|| snapshot.plan_hash.clone())` unconditionally. persist_plan at service.rs:370 sets `snapshot.plan_hash = Some(plan_hash)`, advancing it. On terminal replay after plan evolution: (1) the original completion stored plan_hash=hash_v1; (2) persist_plan updated snapshot to hash_v2; (3) replay with plan_hash=None auto-populates to hash_v2; (4) option_conflicts at fs.rs:3161 compares Some(hash_v1) vs Some(hash_v2) and returns true, triggering incompatible_terminal_update. Pre-change behavior (passing None through) was safe since option_conflicts(Some(x), None) returns false. Both proposed fixes (skip auto-population when entry is already terminal, or weaken the conflict check to backfill-only semantics) are viable.

Verified in code. The runless fallback at fs.rs:3018 accepts a row based only on `entries[*index].run_id.is_none()` plus non-terminal and non-superseded checks — no started_at validation. Meanwhile the non-run_id path at fs.rs:3077-3081 explicitly filters with `entries[*index].started_at == started_at`. The service contract at service.rs:464-469 explicitly documents that started_at is forwarded to the lineage layer so runless retries can replay completion deterministically. Without the started_at guard, a completion targeting attempt A (started at T1) could finalize attempt B (started at T2) if B is the only open runless entry. Adding `entries[*index].started_at == started_at` to the runless fallback guard is a straightforward, correct fix.

Verified in code. This correctly identifies the same root cause as fr-1-0b213bd9 but proposes the complementary fix at the service layer: the `.or_else(|| snapshot.plan_hash.clone())` at service.rs:429-433 and 564-568 is unconditional, replacing None with the current snapshot hash before the adapter ever sees the request. After persist_plan advances snapshot.plan_hash (line 370), any replay of a start or completion for a row recorded under the old plan hash will now carry the new hash, tripping conflict checks at fs.rs:2711-2725 (starts) and fs.rs:3159-3201 (completions). The proposed fix — only auto-populate when the matched row lacks plan_hash rather than blindly at the service boundary — correctly scopes the backfill to its intended purpose. The request for replay-across-plan-update test coverage is well-justified given this is a subtle interaction with no existing coverage.
